== Notes for [[Item:Q116920|Q116920]] ==

Gonçalo Mendes II da Maia , nicknamed The Lidador (c. 1110, Guilhabreu - c. 1139 [ 1 ] ), was a Portuguese aristocrat and knight


===Biography


https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gon%C3%A7alo_Mendes_II_da_Maia

Gonçalo was the son of Mem Soares da Maia , brother of the magnate and head of the family , Paio Soares da Maia , and of an unknown mother. Being the son of a second son, it was unlikely that he would have close relations with the head of the family and/or the government of the family estate, but this does not in fact seem to happen.


Marriage

Gonçalo is said to have married Gontinha Guterres (before 1110-after 1143), patron saint of the Monastery of Rio Tinto , and who appears to have acquired a considerable amount of property in São João da Madeira [ 1 ] . The couple had a daughter, Maria Gonçalves, who married Gonçalo Trastamires, her mother's cousin [ 1 ] . Considering the hypothesis that he was still alive in 1159, it is more than likely that Gontinha preceded him in death, given that the last document confirming this dates from 1143.


Doubtful chronologies



There is no news of this notable baron after 1130. As his two brothers died shortly after the Battle of São Mamede, it is possible to extend Gonçalo's lifetime, at the latest, until 1159 [ 1 ] .

----


"Glorious Death of Gonçalo Mendes da Maia", in a 19th century lithograph.

}

Joaquim Pedro de Aragão (1801-?) - Biblioteca Nacional de Portugal - "Morte Gloriosa de Gonçalo Mendes da Maia" Litografia do século XIX, mostrando a morte heróica de Gonçalo Mendes da Maia < [https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:MorteDeGon%C3%A7aloMendesDaMaia.png link] >

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
